codeReview.summary: The feature adds a clear policy engine, approval queue, and security-audit surface, but it does not securely protect access-mode changes and it resolves approvals before confirming the gated action can still execute.

issues.description: `POST /policy/access-mode` updates the global access mode immediately with no trust, session-auth, or authorization check. Any client that can reach the bridge can switch itself to `full_control`, then approve dangerous actions that were supposed to stay gated, which undermines the policy model this feature is introducing.

Approval resolution mutates the stored approval state before the pending action is executed. If `execute_pending_approval_action` later returns `None` (for example because the thread disappeared at line 1446), the API returns `approval_target_not_found` but the approval has already been marked approved/rejected and is no longer actionable, leaving approval state inconsistent with the underlying operation result.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `security-worker` skill requires a manual fail-closed verification step, but this run recorded only automated `commandsRun` and still marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` as true. Either the skill needs to explicitly allow automated-only validation in this environment or the review/handoff guidance should require workers to record the missing manual check as a deviation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/security-worker/SKILL.md:24 says to manually verify fail-closed behavior and record what the user sees; the handoff at /Users/lubomirmolin/.factory/missions/5e6e6a03-5030-4a14-862e-f91b0b989525/handoffs/2026-03-17T22-49-02-471Z__security-policy-engine-and-access-mode-foundations__98cc869e-5921-4a18-9954-c83de761fa45.json:13-17 only lists automated commands, and :63-65 still marks `followedProcedure` true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared API notes do not yet capture the concrete policy/security surfaces added here (`/policy/access-mode`, approval resolution endpoints, `/security/events`, and the new approval/security event types), so later workers will have to rediscover them from code or transcripts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/.factory/library/api.md:10-18 only documents generic planned surfaces, while crates/bridge-core/src/lib.rs:809-824 lists the concrete policy, approval-resolution, and security-events endpoints now exposed by the bridge.

summary: Reviewed the worker transcript skeleton, handoff, skill, mission docs, and commit `8b56c9e18c9c47504a37af0e1bb43b0c21075049`. Result: fail, due to an unprotected access-mode escalation path; also noted an approval-state consistency edge case and two shared-state gaps.
